name: game-studio
description: Design, prototype, and ship browser games with guided 2D and 3D workflows, asset pipelines, and playtesting support.

interface.shortDescription: Design, prototype, and ship browser games
interface.longDescription: Plan, prototype, and build browser games with guided workflows for gameplay systems, UI, asset pipelines, and playtesting across 2D and 3D projects.
defaultPrompt: Design a browser game and plan the core loop

skillPreviews.name: game-playtest
skillPreviews.description: Run browser-game playtests and frontend QA. Use when the user asks for smoke tests, screenshot-based verification, browser automation, HUD or overlay review, or structured issue-finding in a browser game.

skillPreviews.name: game-studio
skillPreviews.description: Route early browser-game work. Use when the user needs stack selection and workflow planning across design, implementation, assets, and playtesting before moving to a specialist skill.

skillPreviews.name: game-ui-frontend
skillPreviews.description: Design UI surfaces for browser games. Use when the user asks for HUDs, menus, overlays, responsive layouts, or visual direction that must protect the playfield.

skillPreviews.name: phaser-2d-game
skillPreviews.description: Implement 2D browser games with Phaser. Use when the user wants a Phaser, TypeScript, and Vite stack for scenes, gameplay systems, cameras, sprite animation, and DOM-overlay HUD patterns.

skillPreviews.name: react-three-fiber-game
skillPreviews.description: Build React-hosted 3D browser games with React Three Fiber. Use when the user wants pmndrs-based scene composition, shared React state, and 3D HUD integration inside a React app.

skillPreviews.name: sprite-pipeline
skillPreviews.description: Generate and normalize 2D sprite animations. Use when the user asks for full-strip generation from approved source frames, consistent anchor and scale normalization, or preview assets for browser-game animation.

skillPreviews.name: three-webgl-game
skillPreviews.description: Implement browser-game runtimes with plain Three.js. Use when the user wants imperative scene control in TypeScript or Vite with GLB assets, loaders, physics, and low-level WebGL debugging.

skillPreviews.name: web-3d-asset-pipeline
skillPreviews.description: Prepare and optimize browser-game 3D assets. Use when the user asks for GLB or glTF shipping work, including Blender cleanup and export, collision or LOD setup, compression, texture packaging, and runtime validation.

skillPreviews.name: web-game-foundations
skillPreviews.description: Set browser-game architecture before implementation. Use when the user needs engine choice, simulation and render boundaries, input model, asset organization, or save/debug/performance strategy.
